dmp.title: A Curated Corpus of Climate Finance Literature, 1990-2024
dmp.description: Data management plan for a multilingual bibliometric corpus of scholarship around climate finance (1990-2024). Structured following the CNRS template on DMP OPIDoR (Science Europe model). Satisfies CNRS Plan pour la Science Ouverte, Loi Republique numerique (Art. 30), and Plan National pour la Science Ouverte requirements.
dmp.ethical_issues_description: The study uses only publicly available bibliographic metadata; no human subjects were involved (no IRB/CNIL declaration required). No personal data is processed. Author names are public bibliographic metadata. AI tools (sentence-transformers, cross-encoder reranker, LLM extraction) are used for data processing only; their use is documented in the data paper and technical report.

project.title: A Curated Corpus of Climate Finance Literature, 1990-2024
project.description: Construction of a multilingual bibliometric corpus of scholarship around climate finance (1990-2024), merging 6 sources (OpenAlex, ISTEX, Crossref, World Bank, bibCNRS, SciSpace). The corpus supports two publications: a history-of-economics article (Oeconomia) and a data paper (RDJ4HSS).

dataset.title: Climate finance corpus v1.0 - Oeconomia reproducibility archive
dataset.description: Corpus CSV (42,922 rows raw, 31,713 after filtering) bundled with full pipeline source code. Abstracts excluded due to publisher redistribution restrictions. Deposited alongside the Oeconomia manuscript submission.
data_quality_assurance: Six-flag filtering pipeline with full audit trail: missing metadata, absent abstract + irrelevant title, title blacklist, citation isolation, semantic outlier, cross-encoder relevance scoring
Protection criteria override flags: citation count >= 50, multi-source presence, within-corpus citations, teaching syllabus appearance
Cross-validation: citation accuracy 99.0% (n=300), completeness 100% (n=300); metadata title match 99.0% (n=100); embedding validity Cohen's d=0.51 (p<0.001)
Human validation: 100 papers for relevance scoring calibration (AUC = 0.818)
Checksum verification: MD5 checksums for bitwise reproducibility checks
Structural verification: schema, row-count +/-5%, statistical similarity on rebuild

distribution.title: Zenodo deposit v1.0
distribution.description: Reproducibility archive: corpus CSV, embeddings NPZ, citations CSV, pipeline source code, configuration files

host.title: Zenodo
host.description: Zenodo is a general-purpose open repository developed and operated by CERN, enabling researchers to share and preserve research outputs across all fields of science.

dataset.title: Climate finance corpus v1.1 - RDJ4HSS reproducibility archive
dataset.description: Updated corpus bundled with full pipeline source code. Deposited alongside the RDJ4HSS data paper submission. Links to v1.0 as a related record. The corpus CSV includes an in_v1 column for backward compatibility.
data_quality_assurance: Six-flag filtering pipeline with full audit trail
Protection criteria override flags for influential works
Cross-validation on citation accuracy, completeness, and metadata
Human validation for relevance scoring calibration
Checksum and structural verification for reproducibility

distribution.title: Zenodo deposit v1.1
distribution.description: Reproducibility archive: corpus CSV v1.1, embeddings, citations, pipeline source code

host.title: Zenodo
host.description: Zenodo is a general-purpose open repository developed and operated by CERN.

dataset.title: Pipeline source code
dataset.description: Python scripts, YAML configuration, Makefile, DVC pipeline definition. Approximately 150 files. Full pipeline for corpus construction from API harvesting through NLP processing to deposit preparation.
data_quality_assurance: Pre-commit hooks enforce: no commits on main, no secrets, no large files (>500 KB), no conflict markers
Environment pinning: PYTHONHASHSEED=0, SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=0, dependencies locked in pyproject.toml
DVC (Data Version Control) DAG for reproducibility
